skill.name: subagent-driven-development
skill.description: Guides AI coding agents through plan execution with one fresh subagent per task and code review after each task.
skill.summary: Execute independent implementation tasks with reviewed subagent handoffs.
skill.author: DYAI2025

security_audit.summary: Static external command alerts at SKILL.md lines 41, 57, 64, 73, 85, 87, and 111 are false positives with 0.90 confidence because they are fenced workflow examples for subagent prompts, not executable shell or Ruby code. Static weak cryptography alerts at SKILL.md lines 3, 43, 72, and 126 are false positives with 0.92 confidence because no cryptographic algorithm or crypto API appears in those lines. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, network access, filesystem automation, or malicious intent was found.

content.value_statement: Implementation plans can lose quality when one long context handles every task. This skill coordinates fresh subagents, task checkpoints, and code review after each implementation step.
actual_capabilities: Breaks an implementation plan into sequential task execution steps.
Directs a fresh general-purpose subagent to implement each task.
Requires tests and verification before each task is reported complete.
Adds code-review checkpoints between implementation tasks.
Defines handling for critical, important, and minor review issues.
Connects final completion to a separate development branch finishing workflow.
limitations: Works best when the plan already exists and tasks are mostly independent.
Does not provide the referenced code-review or branch-finishing templates.
Can increase tool usage because each task uses a fresh subagent.
Requires human judgment when tasks are tightly coupled or the plan changes.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Feature Delivery
use_cases.description: Run a prepared feature plan through separate implementation and review cycles.

use_cases.title: Keep Long Tasks Organized
use_cases.description: Use fresh task contexts to reduce confusion during multi-step coding work.

use_cases.title: Add Review Gates
use_cases.description: Require code review after each task before moving to the next implementation step.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Simple Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner use with a prepared plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use subagent-driven-development to execute the plan in plans/feature-plan.md. Keep each task separate and review after every task.

prompt_templates.title: Execute with Test Checks
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate use when test results matter.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use subagent-driven-development for this implementation plan. Require each subagent to write or update tests and report the exact verification result.

prompt_templates.title: Handle Review Feedback
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate use with strict quality gates.
prompt_templates.prompt: Execute this plan with subagent-driven-development. Stop after each review, fix critical and important issues, then continue to the next task.

prompt_templates.title: Manage a Complex Branch
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced use across many independent tasks.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use subagent-driven-development for this branch plan. Track base and head commits for each task, request review after each task, and run the final branch completion workflow when all reviews pass.

output_examples.input: Execute a three-task plan for a new settings page.
output: Task 1 implemented and tested by a fresh subagent.
Code review completed with no critical issues.
Task 2 paused until an important review issue is fixed.

output_examples.input: Finish all tasks in a refactor plan.
output: All planned tasks were completed in sequence.
Each task received a review checkpoint.
The final review passed and the branch finishing workflow is ready.

best_practices: Start with a clear written plan before invoking the workflow.
Keep tasks independent enough for separate subagent contexts.
Fix critical and important review findings before continuing.
anti_patterns: Do not use it to create the plan from scratch.
Do not run multiple implementation subagents in parallel on the same branch.
Do not skip review checkpoints to save time.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It guides an AI coding agent through plan execution with fresh subagents and review after each task.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it when you have a written implementation plan with mostly independent tasks.

faq.question: Does it write code itself?
faq.answer: It is a workflow guide. The active agent and dispatched subagents perform the implementation work.

faq.question: Why use a fresh subagent for each task?
faq.answer: A fresh context helps each task follow the plan without carrying unrelated details from earlier work.

faq.question: Does it require code review?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow requires review after each task and a final review after all tasks.

faq.question: Is it suitable for tightly coupled tasks?
faq.answer: No. Tightly coupled work is better handled manually or with a revised plan.
